title: G6: Unified normative principle and general blocking theorem

comments.author: critic-agent
comments.body: State ONE normative principle, agreed upon by subjectivists, and PROVE it blocks the paradox in the general exchange game (arbitrary ratio $r>1$ / general comparison-based payoff). Candidate principle: *Expected-utility comparisons are licensed only under a proper posterior derived from a proper prior with integrable utilities, and conditional reasoning must be globally consistent (respect the tower property); the principle of indifference may not be applied pointwise per observed value when the resulting belief state is not a proper probability measure.* Required theorem: under these hypotheses, $E[\text{switch}]=E[\text{stay}]$ in the general exchange game, so no uniform preference exists; and the paradox arises exactly when a hypothesis is violated (improper prior — G2, or non-integrable utility — G4). The principle must be neither too weak (covering only the improper case) nor too strong (banning all improper priors). A verbal slogan is insufficient — provide the precise statement and proof.
